Social Media Image Sizes

Current image dimensions for every major social media platform and content type — posts, headers, thumbnails, and ads.

Instagram
Width
1080px
Height
1350px
💡 Meta recommends 4:5 portrait in 2026 for maximum reach.
Need to make an image file smaller?Try Image Compressor

Every social media platform has specific image dimension requirements for each content type. Use the wrong size and your image gets cropped, stretched, or pillarboxed in ways you didn't intend. Profile pictures get circular-cropped — if your logo or face isn't centered, the edges will be cut. Cover photos have different display proportions on desktop and mobile. Post images are displayed at different sizes in feed, search, and notifications. Knowing the correct dimensions before you design saves you from repeated exports, surprise cropping, and looking unprofessional. This reference tool keeps all dimensions organized and filterable in one place.

How to use

  1. Browse by platform. Use the platform filter to narrow the list to the social network you're working on. The tool covers Instagram, Facebook, Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube, TikTok, Pinterest, Snapchat, and more.
  2. Find your content type. Each platform has multiple content types — profile picture, cover photo, post image, Story, Reel, ad formats, and video thumbnails. The table shows the recommended dimensions and aspect ratio for each.
  3. Note the file size limits. Many platforms have maximum file size requirements alongside dimension requirements. The table includes both where relevant.
  4. Use the dimensions directly. Once you have the correct pixel dimensions, resize your image using the image resizer or crop to the correct aspect ratio using the image cropper before uploading.

Tips for best results

  • Design at 2× for retina displays. All the dimensions listed are the minimum display sizes. For sharp rendering on retina and high-DPI screens (iPhone, modern Macs, many Android devices), design and export at 2× the dimensions shown — a 1080×1080 Instagram post should be designed as 2160×2160 and let the platform downscale it.
  • Use safe zones for profile pictures. Profile photos are displayed in circles or rounded squares depending on the platform. Any content near the edges of a square profile image may be cropped. Keep the main subject (face, logo) centered and within the central 80% of the frame.
  • Cover photo safe zones differ on mobile and desktop. Platform cover photos are cropped differently on mobile and desktop views. Keep important content (text, logos, faces) in the central portion of the image to ensure they're visible in both contexts.
  • Check platform-specific ad specs separately. Ad sizes change more frequently than organic content sizes as platforms add and retire ad formats. The dimensions here cover organic content; verify ad specs in the platform's official Ads Manager documentation for the current version before creating ad creatives.

Why use PixMidas

  • All platforms in one place. Rather than searching each platform's help documentation separately, all dimensions are organized in a single filterable reference. Find what you need in seconds.
  • Current dimensions. Platform image specs change when platforms redesign their interfaces. This reference is updated to reflect current specifications rather than outdated guides from years ago.
  • No account needed. Free, instant access. No signup, no newsletter, no paywall.

Frequently asked questions

What are the correct Instagram image sizes?

Instagram supports three post aspect ratios: square at 1:1 (minimum 1080×1080 px), portrait at 4:5 (minimum 1080×1350 px), and landscape at 1.91:1 (minimum 1080×566 px). Instagram Stories and Reels use 9:16 (minimum 1080×1920 px). Profile pictures are displayed at 110×110 px on mobile but should be uploaded at at least 320×320 px. Instagram applies quality reduction on images; upload at higher resolution to ensure the final result looks sharp.

What size should a Facebook cover photo be?

Facebook cover photos display at 820×312 px on desktop and 640×360 px on mobile. The recommended upload size is 820×462 px — this size ensures important content is visible on both desktop and mobile without being cropped. Keep key elements (text, logos) away from the left portion of the image since the profile picture overlaps that area on some views.

What is the best size for a Twitter/X header image?

Twitter/X profile headers display at 1500×500 px at a 3:1 aspect ratio. The profile picture overlaps the left-center portion at the bottom of the header. Keep important content away from the left 20% of the header and the bottom edge to avoid overlap. The minimum upload size accepted is 1500×500 px; higher resolutions are down-scaled by the platform.

What size should a YouTube thumbnail be?

YouTube thumbnails should be 1280×720 px (16:9 aspect ratio) at a maximum file size of 2 MB. The minimum is 640×360 px but 1280×720 provides sharp rendering on desktop and mobile. Thumbnails must be under 2 MB in file size — use the image compressor if your thumbnail exceeds this limit.

Do image requirements change frequently?

Platform requirements change when platforms redesign their interfaces or add new content types. Profile picture and cover photo dimensions are relatively stable. Ad format specifications and newer content types (Reels, Stories) change more frequently. Always verify the latest specs in the platform's official creator or advertiser documentation when preparing materials for a campaign or product launch.

Why does my uploaded image look different than what I designed?

Platforms apply their own compression and cropping after upload. Most compress images — sometimes aggressively — which reduces quality and can shift colors slightly. Uploading at higher resolution gives platforms more to work with before compression. Some platforms also enforce aspect ratios by cropping rather than stretching, so if your image doesn't match the expected ratio, edges may be removed. Always use the exact required aspect ratio and test a preview before publishing.

Privacy Note & Disclaimer

PixMidas is designed to process files directly in your browser whenever possible, and we do not intentionally store or inspect your files. However, no online service can guarantee absolute security, so please avoid highly sensitive data. By using PixMidas, you acknowledge that files are processed at your own discretion and risk.
